meta.note: Derived from the repo's WinUI 3 / XAML surfaces. This app themes off Windows Fluent system resources (SystemAccentColor, Mica, Segoe UI Variable), so tokens below map to Fluent theme resources rather than a bespoke hex palette. Edit in the Design System canvas or directly.

brand.name: OpenClaw Windows Hub
brand.tagline: A calm native Windows companion for your AI assistant.
brand.description: OpenClaw Windows Hub is a native Windows companion suite for OpenClaw, the AI-powered personal assistant. Built on WinUI 3 as a system-tray app, it pairs your PC to an OpenClaw gateway, exposes allowlisted Windows node capabilities over MCP, and guides first-run pairing through a setup wizard. Its interface follows Windows Fluent design conventions — Mica surfaces, the user's system accent, and Segoe UI Variable type — so it feels like a quiet, native extension of the OS rather than a bolted-on dashboard.
brand.voice: Clear, respectful, and native to Windows. Explain pairing, permissions, and security plainly. State what will happen and why; never alarmist, never hype.
antiReferences: custom-skinned controls that fight the OS theme
ignoring the user's system accent or light/dark preference
cramming a web dashboard into a desktop window
hard-coded hex that breaks in dark or high-contrast mode

colors.name: ink
colors.usage: Primary text, headings. Fluent TextFillColorPrimary on light.

colors.name: paper
colors.usage: App / page background. Mica base / ApplicationPageBackground (light).

colors.name: accent
colors.usage: Primary actions, links, selection, focus. Follows SystemAccentColor (default AccentDark1 on light).

display.family: "Segoe UI Variable Display", "Segoe UI Variable", "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif
display.usage: Page and section titles. Segoe UI Variable Display optical size.

body.family: "Segoe UI Variable Text", "Segoe UI Variable", "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif
body.usage: Body copy, UI labels, buttons. Segoe UI Variable Text optical size.

mono.usage: Commands, endpoints, tokens, logs, timestamps. Used heavily across setup and diagnostics surfaces.

radii.name: sm
radii.usage: Buttons, inputs, small controls. Fluent ControlCornerRadius.

principles: Defer to Windows. Theme off SystemAccentColor and Fluent resources so the app follows the user's accent, light/dark, and high-contrast choices.
One accent, and it means "act here". Selection, focus, and primary actions share the system accent; don't spend it on decoration.
Hierarchy over decoration — size, weight, and space do the work before color does.
State must be legible: connected, pairing, and error states read clearly via positive/warning/critical, never color alone.
No hard-coded hex in UI. Use tokens/theme resources so dark and high-contrast modes stay correct.
Motion is a cue, not a garnish: 120-200ms ease-out, no bounce.
